steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(21) = \frac{4}{21}.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that function $f$ is defined by the equation $f\left(x\right)=4{x}^{-1}$. The value of $f\left(21\right)$ is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=21$. Substituting $21$ for $x$ in the given equation yields $f\left(21\right)=4{\left(21\right)}^{-1}$, which is equivalent to $f\left(21\right)=4\left(\frac{1}{21}\right)$, or $f\left(21\right)=\frac{4}{21}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(21\right)$ when $f\left(x\right)=-4x$, rather than $f\left(x\right)=4{x}^{-1}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(21\right)$ when $f\left(x\right)={\left(4x\right)}^{-1}$, rather than $f\left(x\right)=4{x}^{-1}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(21\right)$ when $f\left(x\right)=\left({4}^{-1}\right)x$, rather than $f\left(x\right)=4{x}^{-1}$.
